chains.oneLiner: Everyone knows NVDA. Pros own the rest of the stack.
chains.whyItMatters: AI is the biggest theme of the decade. The obvious play (Nvidia) has rallied 10x — but the second-order beneficiaries are still under-owned.

layers.name: AI product makers
layers.why: Headline names. Already priced for perfection.

layers.name: Chip foundries
layers.why: Every AI chip in the world is fabricated by ~3 companies.

layers.name: Fab equipment — picks and shovels
layers.why: Without these machines, no fab makes chips. ASML's EUV has zero competitors.

layers.name: Raw materials
layers.why: Silicon wafers, copper, rare earths — physical inputs to every chip.

layers.name: Power for AI
layers.why: AI data centers need 10× more power. Utilities are quietly an AI play.

retailVsPro.retail: Buys NVDA at all-time highs after a 200% rally. Often top-ticks.
retailVsPro.pro: Bought ASML + copper + nuclear utilities 18 months ago.

chains.oneLiner: The car is the headline. The battery is the moat.
chains.whyItMatters: EV makers fight a brutal price war. Battery, mining, charging layers have less competition and longer-tail demand.

layers.name: EV makers
layers.why: Brutal price war. Hard to pick winners.

layers.name: Battery makers
layers.why: 40% of EV cost is the battery. ~5 Asian giants dominate.

layers.name: Battery materials
layers.why: Lithium, cobalt, nickel — supply concentrated, hard to scale fast.

layers.name: Charging infrastructure
layers.why: EVs useless without chargers. Toll-road economics.

layers.name: Power grid + copper
layers.why: EVs strain the grid. New transformers, more copper.

retailVsPro.pro: Quietly accumulates lithium miners benefiting regardless of which EV wins.

chains.oneLiner: When the strait shakes, gold and oil rise.
chains.whyItMatters: 20% of global oil ships through Hormuz. Every flare-up triggers the same chain — flight to safety, energy spike, defense rallies.

layers.name: Direct safe-havens
layers.why: Centuries-old behaviour. Fear up = gold up = USD up.

layers.name: Energy spike
layers.why: Hormuz disruption = global oil shortage.

layers.name: Defense ramp
layers.why: Conflict = more weapons orders. Defense budgets ratchet up.

layers.name: Shipping & insurance
layers.why: Tankers reroute, insurance spikes, both lift maritime stocks.

retailVsPro.retail: Sees war headlines, panics, sells at the bottom.
retailVsPro.pro: Holds permanent gold + oil allocation as 'crisis insurance.'

chains.oneLiner: The most predictable demographic shift in history.
chains.whyItMatters: By 2030, 1 in 5 Americans will be 65+. Healthcare spend, retirement income demand, senior housing all surge. Trend doesn't care about recessions.

layers.name: Big pharma
layers.why: Older population = more meds.

layers.name: Hospitals & insurers
layers.why: Per-person healthcare spend doubles past 65.

layers.name: Medical devices
layers.why: Hip replacements, hearing aids, glucose monitors. Recurring revenue.

layers.name: Senior REITs
layers.why: Assisted living. Demand growing, supply constrained.

retailVsPro.retail: Chases pharma during scares. Misses the 30-year compound.
retailVsPro.pro: Holds healthcare ETF + senior REITs as multi-decade hold.

chains.oneLiner: Solar panels are obvious. The grid behind them isn't.
chains.whyItMatters: $5T global investment going into clean energy this decade. Pros own the boring grid + materials layer where margins are protected.

layers.name: Solar/wind makers
layers.why: Panel prices fall yearly. Brutal Chinese competition.

layers.name: Grid build-out
layers.why: Renewables need new transmission, smart meters, storage.

layers.name: Critical materials
layers.why: Polysilicon for solar, rare earths for wind, copper for grids.

layers.name: Nuclear renaissance
layers.why: AI data centers reviving nuclear. Backed by MSFT, AMZN.

retailVsPro.retail: Buys solar ETFs at peak, gets crushed in panel-price crashes.
retailVsPro.pro: Owns grid contractors + uranium + copper as transition picks-and-shovels.

chains.oneLiner: AI hype is loud. The buildings storing AI are quiet.
chains.whyItMatters: Every AI model runs in a data center. Demand growing 30%/year. Construction, cooling, networking, real estate around centers — less crowded than NVDA.

layers.name: Hyperscalers
layers.why: MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL spending $250B/year combined.

layers.name: Data center REITs
layers.why: Own the actual buildings. Long lease contracts, recurring rent.

layers.name: Cooling + power gear
layers.why: AI chips run hot. Liquid cooling now standard.

layers.name: Power generation
layers.why: Single AI data center = power of a small city.

retailVsPro.retail: Buys NVDA, calls it 'AI play,' done.
retailVsPro.pro: Owns Equinix + Vertiv + utility — captures AI demand at every layer.
